DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sara G. Noel, Esq. of FILICE BROWN EASSA & McLEOD LLP (510-444-3131), appearing for defendant / moving party Chevron U.S.A. Inc.
Grant Walters, Esq. of CLAPPER, PATTI, SCHWEIZER & MASON (415-332-4262), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Martha Harper, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Chevron U.S.A. Inc.'s demurrer TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Matter was argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's demurrer to first amended complaint as to causes of action 1-7 and 15 is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The phrase 'asbestos-containing products' fails to sufficiently identify a product to which the decedent was exposed as required by Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 80. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christine A. Renken, Esq. of CLAPPER, PATTI SCHWEIZER & MASON (415-332-4262), appearing for defendant / moving party Exxon Mobil Corporation
No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's demurrer to plaintiffs' first amended complaint for damages. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is overruled. Plaintiffs' first amended complaint states valid causes of action for conspiracy against Exxon. Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled that Exxon had a duty to plaintiffs, individually and not as a conspirator (Chavers v. Gatke Corp. (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 606, 614), and that Exxon conspired to engage in wrongful acts that harmed plaintiffs (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 511). Exxon's objection to the late filing of plaintiffs' opposition is overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
